title_en: Al-Bayhaqi and the Concept of the Branches of Faith
content_id:
Ahmad ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali al-Bayhaqi lahir pada tahun 384 H di Bayhaq, sebuah distrik di kawasan Khorasan di dunia Islam timur. Beliau menjadi ulama hadis dan sistematikus paling produktif pada abad kelima kalender Islam, menghasilkan karya dengan cakupan yang luar biasa yang mencakup seluruh rentang ilmu-ilmu hadis di samping keterlibatan mendalam dengan fikih Islam dari perspektif Syafi'i. Shu'ab al-Iman beliau — Cabang-cabang Iman — adalah di antara karya-karyanya yang paling ambisius, baik dalam konsep organisasinya maupun dalam ukurannya yang sangat besar.

Konsep pengorganisasian Shu'ab al-Iman berasal dari sebuah tradisi kenabian yang terkenal, yang dilaporkan dalam Sahih al-Bukhari, yang menyatakan bahwa iman memiliki lebih dari tujuh puluh cabang — beberapa riwayat menyebut tujuh puluh tujuh — yang tertinggi adalah pernyataan bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah, dan yang terendah adalah menyingkirkan sesuatu yang berbahaya dari jalan. Al-Bayhaqi mengambil tradisi ini sebagai kerangka organisasinya dan berusaha mengidentifikasi dan mendeskripsikan setiap cabang iman, mengumpulkan bukti hadis yang berkaitan dengan masing-masing. Hasilnya adalah sebuah teologi Islam yang komprehensif tentang praktik yang disajikan melalui lensa iman dan manifestasi perilakunya.

Shu'ab al-Iman adalah salah satu koleksi hadis terbesar yang pernah disusun. Edisi cetak modern mencakup empat belas atau lebih jilid yang substansial, memuat ribuan hadis yang diorganisir ke dalam lebih dari tiga ratus bab yang sesuai dengan cabang-cabang iman yang diidentifikasi al-Bayhaqi. Cakupan karya ini mencerminkan pemahaman al-Bayhaqi bahwa iman dalam Islam bukan sekadar masalah keyakinan batin tetapi mencakup setiap dimensi praktik, dari afirmasi teologis tertinggi hingga tindakan kehidupan sehari-hari yang paling biasa.

Al-Bayhaqi adalah seorang Syafi'i yang teguh dan pengikut tradisi teologi Asy'ari yang dikaitkan dengan Abu al-Hasan al-Asy'ari. Komitmen-komitmen ini membentuk baik pemilihan maupun penyajian materi dalam Shu'ab, meskipun tidak mencegahnya dari terlibat dengan posisi-posisi mazhab lain dengan keadilan dan keakuratan yang cukup besar.
content_en:
Ahmad ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali al-Bayhaqi was born in 384 AH in Bayhaq, a district in the Khorasan region of the eastern Islamic world. He became the most prolific and systematic hadith scholar of the fifth century of the Islamic calendar, producing an output of extraordinary scope that covered the full range of the hadith sciences alongside deep engagement with Islamic jurisprudence from the Shafi'i perspective. His Shu'ab al-Iman — Branches of Faith — is among his most ambitious works, both in its organizational concept and in its sheer size.

The organizing concept of Shu'ab al-Iman derives from a celebrated prophetic tradition, reported in Sahih al-Bukhari, which states that faith has over seventy branches — some accounts say seventy-seven — the highest of which is the declaration that there is no god but God, and the lowest of which is removing something harmful from a path. Al-Bayhaqi took this tradition as his organizing framework and set out to identify and describe each of the branches of faith, gathering the hadith evidence related to each one. The result is a comprehensive theology of Islamic practice presented through the lens of faith and its behavioral manifestations.

The Shu'ab al-Iman is one of the largest hadith collections ever compiled. Modern printed editions run to fourteen or more substantial volumes, containing thousands of hadiths organized into over three hundred chapters corresponding to the branches of faith that al-Bayhaqi identified. The scope of the work reflects al-Bayhaqi's understanding that faith (iman) in Islam is not merely a matter of interior belief but encompasses every dimension of practice, from the highest theological affirmations to the most ordinary acts of daily life.

Al-Bayhaqi was a committed Shafi'i and a follower of the Ash'ari theological tradition associated with Abu al-Hasan al-Ash'ari. These commitments shaped both his selection and presentation of material in the Shu'ab, though they did not prevent him from engaging with the positions of other schools with considerable fairness and accuracy.
content_ar: أحمد ibn al-Husayn ibn علي al-Bayhaqi was born in 384 AH in Bayhaq, a district in the Khorasan region of the eastern Islamic الدنيا. He became the most prolific and systematic الحديث scholar of the خامساً century of the Islamic calendar, producing an output of extraordinary scope that covered the full range of the الحديث sciences alongside deep engagement with Islamic jurisprudence from the الشافعي perspective. His Shu'ab al-الإيمان — Branches of الإيمان — is among his most ambitious works, both in its organizational concept and in its sheer size. The organizing concept of Shu'ab al-الإيمان derives from a celebrated prophetic التراث, reported in صحيح البخاري, which states that الإيمان has over seventy branches — some accounts say seventy-seven — the highest of which is the declaration that there is no god but God, and the lowest of which is removing something harmful from a الطريق. Al-Bayhaqi took this التراث as his organizing framework and set out to identify and describe each of the branches of الإيمان, gathering the الحديث evidence related to each one. The result is a comprehensive theology of Islamic practice presented through the lens of الإيمان and its behavioral manifestations. The Shu'ab al-الإيمان is one of the largest الحديث collections ever compiled. Modern printed editions run to fourteen or more substantial volumes, containing thousands of hadiths organized into over three hundred الأبواب corresponding to the branches of الإيمان that al-Bayhaqi identified. The scope of the work reflects al-Bayhaqi's الفهم that الإيمان (الإيمان) in الإسلام is not merely a matter of interior الاعتقاد but encompasses every dimension of practice, from the highest theological affirmations to the most ordinary acts of daily الحياة. Al-Bayhaqi was a committed الشافعي and a follower of the الأشعري theological التراث associated with Abu al-حسن al-الأشعري. These commitments shaped both his selection and presentation of material in the Shu'ab, though they did not prevent him from engaging with the positions of other schools with considerable fairness and accuracy.
